properties.remark: LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTS A FEW TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN AND DAMAGE TO FENCE AND ROOF ALONG STAHLMAN AVENUE. TIME ESTIMATED VIA RADAR.
49 MPH WIND GUST RECORDED AT THE PENSACOLA REGIONAL AIRPORT WITHIN ON MILE OF THE COAST.
65MPH WIND GUST AT THE MOSSY COVE WEATHERFLOW SITE ON THE BAY NEAR AXELSON POINT.
44MPH WIND GUST AT THE OKALOOSA ISLAND FISHING PIER XOPF WEATHERFLOW STATION AT FORT WALTON BEACH.

EMERGENCY MANAGER RELAYED A REPORT OF DAMAGE TO A RESIDENCE NEAR LATON HILL. TREES DOWN WITH ONE TREE THAT FELL IN A WINDOW, PART OF THE ROOF OF THE RESIDENCE SEVERELY DAMAGED AND A SHED BLOWN OVER. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

NWS SURVEY DETERMINED THAT A BRIEF EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST SOUTH OF HWY 56 AND EAST OF FLAT BRANCH RD. THE TORNADO RESULTED IN DAMAGE TO THE METAL ROOF OF A RESIDENCE ALONG WITH ONE WINDOW BLOWN OUT. SCATTERED SOFTWOOD TREE DAMAGE WAS NOTED ALONG THE SHORT PATH. ESTIMATED 75-80 MPH.

WLOX REPORTS AT LEAST 70 PEOPLE HAVE BEEN CONFIRMED RESCUED FROM THE RED CREEK OFF ROAD RV CAMPGROUND ON VESTRY RD DUE TO FLOODING FROM RED CREEK.

A BRIEF EF-0 TORNADO OCCURRED ON CRABTREE CHURCH RD. ONE HOME EXPERIENCED SHINGLE DAMAGE AND A BROKEN WINDOW AND A MOBILE HOME WAS SHIFTED ON ITS FOUNDATION. THERE WERE SEVERAL SMALL TREES UPROOTED AND NUMEROUS LARGE BRANCHES BROKEN. MAXIMUM WINDS ESTIMATED AROUND 85 MPH.
AT LEAST 5 STRUCTURES WITH DAMAGE AND SEVERAL DOWNED TREES AND POWER LINES ALONG CRABTREE CHURCH ROAD. REPORTS AND PICTURES VIA NORTHESCAMBIA ONLINE NEWSPAPER AND SOCIAL MEDIA.

AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR JEFFCOAT RD. ONE MOBILE HOME AND A SHED WERE DESTROYED. SEVERAL LARGE TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED, ONE OF WHICH FELL ON A VEHICLE. THE TORNADO THEN CAUSED SPORADIC DAMAGE AS IT PASSED THROUGH MAINLY WOODED AREAS UPROOTING AND SNAPPING LARGE TREES, WITH ONE FALLING ON A HOUSE AND CAUSING MINOR DAMAGE. MAXIMUM WINDS ESTIMATED AROUND 95 MPH. THE TORNADO LIFTED NEAR CENTER RIDGE ROAD.
SEVERAL TREES SNAPPED AND UPROOTED IN THE SELLERSVILLE COMMUNITY. PICTURES VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.
SIDING AND ROOF DAMAGE TO A HOUSE ON FUQUA RD WITH DAMAGE TO A NEARBY BARN. PICTURES VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.
METAL ROOF BLOWN OFF A HOME AND TREE BRANCHES DOWN IN THE BETHEL COMMUNITY SOUTHEAST OF BREWTON, AL
METAL ROOF BLOWN OFF A HOME AND TREE BRANCHES DOWN IN THE BETHEL COMMUNITY SOUTHEAST OF BREWTON, AL.

NWS SURVEY DETERMINED THAT A SEVERE THUNDERSTORM PRODUCED SPORADIC STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE. SEVERAL LARGE TREES WERE UPROOTED ALONG RAINTREE RD AND YELLOW CREEK RD. SEVERAL TREES WERE ALSO SNAPPED IN HALF AND LAYING IN THE ADJACENT FIELD. MINOR DAMAGE TO A SHED AND ROOF OF A MANUFACTURED HOME ALONG YELLOW CREEK RD. ESTIMATED 75-80 MPH.

NWS SURVEY DETERMINED THAT A SEVERE THUNDERSTORM PRODUCED SPORADIC STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE. SEVERAL LARGE TREES WERE UPROOTED ALONG RAINTREE RD AND YELLOW CREEK RD. SEVERAL TREES WERE ALSO SNAPPED IN HALF AND LAYING IN THE ADJACENT FIELD. MINOR DAMAGE TO A SHED AND ROOF OF A MANUFACTURED HOME ALONG YELLOW CREEK RD. ESTIMATED 75-80 MPH. THE DURATION OF THE TSTM WND DMG EVENT WAS 2 MINUTES.

REPORTS OF DAMAGE TO A MOBILE HOME AND ALONG YELLOW CREEK ROAD NEAR THE COMMUNITY OF ROBERTS, ALABAMA.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR DANIEL MILL RD CAUSING MINOR TREE DAMAGE, THEN STRENGTHENED TO AN EF-1 BEFORE REACHING OLIVER MYERS RD. SEVERAL LARGE TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED. THE TORNADO CAUSED MINOR TO MODERATE ROOF AND SIDING DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES AROUND A LAKE IN THE MAGNOLIA SHORES AREA. MAXIMUM WINDS ESTIMATED AROUND 100 MPH.
PICTURES OF TREES SNAPPED AND NUMEROUS LARGE BRANCHES DOWN NEAR A HOME IN THE AREA OF HIGHLAND HOME AND MAGNOLIA SPRINGS.
PICTURES OF TREES SNAPPED AND NUMEROUS LARGE BRANCHES DOWN NEAR A HOME IN THE AREA OF HIGHLAND HOME AND MAGNOLIA SPRINGS.

A STRONG WATERSPOUT MOVED ONSHORE AT THE 2200 BLOCK OF WEST BEACH BLVD. THE EF-1 TORNADO PRODUCED ROOF AND SIDING DAMAGE TO TWO HOMES. SEVERAL WINDOWS WERE ALSO BLOWN OUT. MAXIMUM WINDS ESTIMATED AROUND 95 MPH.
MINOR DAMAGE TO A BUILDING AND POWERLINES DOWN ALONG WEST BEACH BLVD.
MINOR DAMAGE TO A BUILDING AND POWERLINES DOWN ALONG WEST BEACH BLVD.
SEVEN OVER TURNED TRAILERS AT ANCHORS RV PARK. MINOR DAMAGE TO A LOWES PARKING LOT.
SEVEN OVER TURNED TRAILERS AT ANCHORS RV PARK. MINOR DAMAGE TO A LOWES PARKING LOT.

*** 3 INJ *** AN EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AT THE ANCHORS AWEIGH RV RESORT WHERE 5 RVS WERE OVERTURNED. 3 PEOPLE INSIDE THE RVS WERE INJURED. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST TO JUST EAST OF HWY 59 AND CR-20. MINOR DAMAGE TO A CONVENIENCE STORY CANOPY AND TO THE LOWE'S WERE OBSERVED. THE TORNADO LIFTED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF PRIDE DR AND JUNIPER ST. MAXIMUM WINDS ESTIMATED AROUND 80 MPH.
TREES AND BRANCHES DOWN ALONG HWY 87 NORTH OF HWY 98 NEAR ELBERTA. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
TREES AND BRANCHES DOWN ALONG HWY 87 NORTH OF HWY 98 NEAR ELBERTA. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
MILL POINT XMLP WEATHERFLOW SITE OBSERVED A WIND GUST OF 39MPH THIS AFTERNOON.

AN EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF CHATELAINE DR AND CR-87. A FEW HOMES EXPERIENCED SHINGLE DAMAGE. SOME TREES UPROOTED. THE TORNADO CONTINUED MOVING ALMOST DUE NORTH, PARALLELING CR-87, AND LIFTED NORTH OF THE DIXIE RD INTERSECTION. ISOLATED INSTANCES OF VERY MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO A FEW RESIDENCES. MAXIMUM WINDS ESTIMATED AROUND 80 MPH.
LARGE BRANCHES SNAPPED AND A FENCE BLOWN OVER IN THE BROOKSIDE HILLS SUBDIVISON.
LARGE BRANCHES SNAPPED AND A FENCE BLOWN OVER IN THE BROOKSIDE HILLS SUBDIVISON.
VIDEO OF A WATERSPOUT MOVING ONSHORE ALONG FORT WALTON BEACH.
VIDEO OF A WATERSPOUT MOVING ONSHORE ALONG FORT WALTON BEACH.

*** 2 INJ *** CORRECTS PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM 2 S FORT WALTON BEACH. THE EF-1 TORNADO MOVED ASHORE ON OKALOOSA ISLAND AND PRODUCED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO A HOME ON SAILFISH DRIVE. THE TORNADO THEN MOVED ACROSS SANTA ROSA SOUND AND INTO FORT WALTON BEACH, PRODUCING TREE DAMAGE AND BROKEN WINDOWS AT ST MARY CATHOLIC CHURCH AND SCHOOL. ROOF DAMAGE TO SEVERAL UNITS IN AN APARTMENT COMPLEX WAS NOTED. 2 MINOR INJURIES REPORTED. MAXIMUM WINDS ESTIMATED BETWEEN 100-105 MPH. THE TORNADO LIFTED JUST NORTH OF JET DRIVE.

*** 2 INJ *** THE EF-1 TORNADO MOVED ASHORE ON OKALOOSA ISLAND AND PRODUCED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO A HOME ON SAILFISH DRIVE. THE TORNADO THEN MOVED ACROSS SANTA ROSA SOUND AND INTO FORT WALTON BEACH, PRODUCING TREE DAMAGE AND BROKEN WINDOWS AT ST MARY CATHOLIC CHURCH AND SCHOOL. ROOF DAMAGE TO SEVERAL UNITS IN AN APARTMENT COMPLEX WAS NOTED. 2 MINOR INJURIES REPORTED. THE TORNADO LIFTED JUST NORTH OF JET DRIVE.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND SPORADIC DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH 60 TO 70 MPH STRAIGHT LINE WINDS SOUTH OF THREE NOTCH ROAD BETWEEN AIRPORT RD AND MCDONALD RD. A FEW HOMES IN THE DAWES OAK SUBDIVISION HAD SHINGLE DAMAGE WITH NUMEROUS FENCES DAMAGED. SPORADIC, MOSTLY MINOR, TREE DAMAGE WAS NOTED IN THE AREA.
